apt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Apatani.
cap: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chipaya.
capacity: noun. The ability to hold, receive, or absorb.
cat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Catalan.
cay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cayuga.
city: noun. A large settlement, bigger than a town; sometimes with a specific legal definition, depending on the place.
icy: adjective. Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice; cold; frosty.
pac: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pacoh.
paca: noun. Any of the large rodents of the genus Cuniculus (but see also its synonyms), native to Central America and South America, which have
pact: noun. An agreement; a compact; a covenant.
pat: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Papitalai.
pay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pech.
pia: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pima Bajo.
pic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pinji.
pica: noun. A disorder characterized by appetite and craving for non-edible substances, such as chalk, clay, dirt, ice, or sand.
pit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pitta-Pitta.
pita: noun. A piece of pita bread; a flatbread pouch used for making sandwiches such as gyros or falafels.
pity: noun. A feeling of sympathy at the misfortune or suffering of someone or something.
pya: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Burmese kyat.
